verses.kjv: PAUL, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:
verses.bsb: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy,  To the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:

verses.kjv: Grace unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.kjv: We are bound to thank God always for you, brethren, as it is meet, because that your faith groweth exceedingly, and the charity of every one of you all toward each other aboundeth;
verses.bsb: We are obligated to thank God for you all the time, brothers, as is fitting, because your faith is growing more and more, and your love for one another is increasing.

verses.kjv: So that we ourselves glory in you in the churches of God for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations that ye endure:
verses.bsb: That is why we boast among God’s churches about your perseverance and faith in the face of all the persecution and affliction you are enduring.

verses.kjv: Which is a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God, that ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, for which ye also suffer:
verses.bsb: All this is clear evidence of God’s righteous judgment. And so you will be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, for which you are suffering.

verses.kjv: Seeing it is a righteous thing with God to recompense tribulation to them that trouble you;
verses.bsb: After all, it is only right for God to repay with affliction those who afflict you,

verses.kjv: And to you who are troubled rest with us, when the Lord Jesus shall be revealed from heaven with his mighty angels,
verses.bsb: and to grant relief to you who are oppressed and to us as well. This will take place when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with His mighty angels

verses.kjv: In flaming fire taking vengeance on them that know not God, and that obey not the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ:
verses.bsb: in blazing fire, inflicting vengeance on those who do not know God and do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.

verses.kjv: Who shall be punished with everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord, and from the glory of his power;
verses.bsb: They will suffer the penalty of eternal destruction, separated from the presence of the Lord and the glory of His might,

verses.kjv: When he shall come to be glorified in his saints, and to be admired in all them that believe (because our testimony among you was believed) in that day.
verses.bsb: on the day He comes to be glorified in His saints and regarded with wonder by all who have believed, including you who have believed our testimony.

verses.kjv: Wherefore also we pray always for you, that our God would count you worthy of this calling, and fulfil all the good pleasure of his goodness, and the work of faith with power:
verses.bsb: To this end, we always pray for you, that our God will count you worthy of His calling, and that He will powerfully fulfill your every good desire and work of faith,

verses.kjv: That the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and ye in him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: so that the name of our Lord Jesus will be glorified in you, and you in Him, according to the grace of our God and of the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.kjv: NOW we beseech you, brethren, by the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, and by our gathering together unto him,
verses.bsb: Now concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered together to Him, we ask you, brothers,

verses.kjv: That ye be not soon shaken in mind, or be troubled, neither by spirit, nor by word, nor by letter as from us, as that the day of Christ is at hand.
verses.bsb: not to be easily disconcerted or alarmed by any spirit or message or letter seeming to be from us, alleging that the Day of the Lord has already come.

verses.kjv: Let no man deceive you by any means: for that day shall not come, except there come a falling away first, and that man of sin be revealed, the son of perdition;
verses.bsb: Let no one deceive you in any way, for it will not come until the rebellion occurs and the man of lawlessness—the son of destruction—is revealed.

verses.kjv: Who opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God, or that is worshipped; so that he as God sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God.
verses.bsb: He will oppose and exalt himself above every so-called god or object of worship. So he will seat himself in the temple of God, proclaiming himself to be God.

verses.kjv: Remember ye not, that, when I was yet with you, I told you these things?
verses.bsb: Do you not remember that I told you these things while I was still with you?

verses.kjv: And now ye know what withholdeth that he might be revealed in his time.
verses.bsb: And you know what is now restraining him, so that he may be revealed at the proper time.

verses.kjv: For the mystery of iniquity doth already work: only he who now letteth will let, until he be taken out of the way.
verses.bsb: For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work, but the one who now restrains it will continue until he is taken out of the way.

verses.kjv: And then shall that Wicked be revealed, whom the Lord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth, and shall destroy with the brightness of his coming:
verses.bsb: And then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord Jesus will slay with the breath of His mouth and annihilate by the majesty of His arrival.

verses.kjv: Even him, whose coming is after the working of Satan with all power and signs and lying wonders,
verses.bsb: The coming of the lawless one will be accompanied by the working of Satan, with every kind of power, sign, and false wonder,

verses.kjv: And with all deceivableness of unrighteousness in them that perish; because they received not the love of the truth, that they might be saved.
verses.bsb: and with every wicked deception directed against those who are perishing, because they refused the love of the truth that would have saved them.

verses.kjv: And for this cause God shall send them strong delusion, that they should believe a lie:
verses.bsb: For this reason God will send them a powerful delusion so that they believe the lie,

verses.kjv: That they all might be damned who believed not the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness.
verses.bsb: in order that judgment may come upon all who have disbelieved the truth and delighted in wickedness.

verses.kjv: But we are bound to give thanks alway to God for you, brethren beloved of the Lord, because God hath from the beginning chosen you to salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief of the truth:
verses.bsb: But we should always thank God for you, brothers who are loved by the Lord, because God has chosen you from the beginning to be saved by the sanctification of the Spirit and by faith in the truth.

verses.kjv: Whereunto he called you by our gospel, to the obtaining of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: To this He called you through our gospel, so that you may share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.kjv: Therefore, brethren, stand fast, and hold the traditions which ye have been taught, whether by word, or our epistle.
verses.bsb: Therefore, brothers, stand firm and cling to the traditions we taught you, whether by speech or by letter.

verses.kjv: Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God, even our Father, which hath loved us, and hath given us everlasting consolation and good hope through grace,
verses.bsb: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ Himself and God our Father, who by grace has loved us and given us eternal comfort and good hope,

verses.kjv: Comfort your hearts, and stablish you in every good word and work.
verses.bsb: encourage your hearts and strengthen you in every good word and deed.

verses.kjv: FINALLY, brethren, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may have free course, and be glorified, even as it is with you:
verses.bsb: Finally, brothers, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may spread quickly and be held in honor, just as it was with you.

verses.kjv: And that we may be delivered from unreasonable and wicked men: for all men have not faith.
verses.bsb: And pray that we may be delivered from wicked and evil men; for not everyone holds to the faith.

verses.kjv: But the Lord is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep you from evil.
verses.bsb: But the Lord is faithful, and He will strengthen you and guard you from the evil one.

verses.kjv: And we have confidence in the Lord touching you, that ye both do and will do the things which we command you.
verses.bsb: And we have confidence in the Lord that you are doing and will continue to do what we command.

verses.kjv: And the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God, and into the patient waiting for Christ.
verses.bsb: May the Lord direct your hearts into God’s love and Christ’s perseverance.

verses.kjv: Now we command you, brethren, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye withdraw yourselves from every brother that walketh disorderly, and not after the tradition which he received of us.
verses.bsb: Now we command you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to keep away from any brother who leads an undisciplined life that is not in keeping with the tradition you received from us.

verses.kjv: For yourselves know how ye ought to follow us: for we behaved not ourselves disorderly among you;
verses.bsb: For you yourselves know how you ought to imitate us, because we were not undisciplined among you,

verses.kjv: Neither did we eat any man’s bread for nought; but wrought with labour and travail night and day, that we might not be chargeable to any of you:
verses.bsb: nor did we eat anyone’s food without paying for it. Instead, in labor and toil, we worked night and day so that we would not be a burden to any of you.

verses.kjv: Not because we have not power, but to make ourselves an ensample unto you to follow us.
verses.bsb: Not that we lack this right, but we wanted to offer ourselves as an example for you to imitate.

verses.kjv: For even when we were with you, this we commanded you, that if any would not work, neither should he eat.
verses.bsb: For even while we were with you, we gave you this command: “If anyone is unwilling to work, he shall not eat.”

verses.kjv: For we hear that there are some which walk among you disorderly, working not at all, but are busybodies.
verses.bsb: Yet we hear that some of you are leading undisciplined lives and accomplishing nothing but being busybodies.

verses.kjv: Now them that are such we command and exhort by our Lord Jesus Christ, that with quietness they work, and eat their own bread.
verses.bsb: We command and urge such people by our Lord Jesus Christ to begin working quietly to earn their own living.

verses.kjv: But ye, brethren, be not weary in well doing.
verses.bsb: But as for you, brothers, do not grow weary in well-doing.

verses.kjv: And if any man obey not our word by this epistle, note that man, and have no company with him, that he may be ashamed.
verses.bsb: Take note of anyone who does not obey the instructions we have given in this letter. Do not associate with him, so that he may be ashamed.

verses.kjv: Yet count him not as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.
verses.bsb: Yet do not regard him as an enemy, but warn him as a brother.

verses.kjv: Now the Lord of peace himself give you peace always by all means. The Lord be with you all.
verses.bsb: Now may the Lord of peace Himself give you peace at all times and in every way. The Lord be with all of you.

verses.kjv: The salutation of Paul with mine own hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I write.
verses.bsb: This greeting is in my own hand—Paul. This is my mark in every letter; it is the way I write.

verses.kjv: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen. ¶ The second epistle to the Thessalonians was written from Athens.
verses.bsb: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
